title: Word Definitions, Tokenization, Boundaries, and Offsets · Runtime Lens: Token Offsets
summary: Whitespace splitting treats punctuation as words, a regex drops apostrophes and non-Latin scripts, hyphen policy changes totals silently, and empty tokens enter the frequency table. Predict the user decision, input identity, text policy, expected report, failure boundary, and evidence that could disprove the proposed implementation before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the token offsets increment in tested word and token contract. Learners progress through predic…
objectives: Return original start and end offsets with normalized token keys so count evidence can be inspected in source.
Specify leading, trailing, internal, curly-apostrophe, dash, and repeated-punctuation behavior with changed examples.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-bookbot-python cumulative Unicode-safe text-analysis portfolio.

Browser execution uses original fixed in-memory fixtures and deterministic pure-Python models only.
Host files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, package installation, native profiling, load,
operating-system races, publication, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class TextPolicy:
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    normalization: str = "NFC"
    casefold: bool = True
    top_k: int = 10


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    corpus_id: str
    revision: str
    policy_version: str
    command_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Bookbot portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Worked focus: bookbot-token-offsets — Return original start and end offsets with normalized token keys so count evidence can be inspected in source.
# Evidence: bookbot-worked-bookbot-tokenization-word-policy-2
# Competency: bookbot-worked-bookbot-tokenization-word-policy-2.
# Case 7417: an assistive-technology terminal review.
# Operating constraint: record the first failed boundary, causal evidence, repair, regression test, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner.
# Changed case: replace an ASCII word with a canonically equivalent combining sequence and compare the declared counts.
def worked_bookbot_tokenization_word_policy_2(text_length: int = 42, token_count: int = 7, empty_tokens: int = 0, offsets_valid: bool = True, word_policy_named: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked2 = "worked2-2dekrow-7417"
    assert evidence_variant_worked2
    evidence_axes_worked2 = (
        "corpusworked2",
        "policy2dekrow",
        "revision7417",
        "commandworked27417",
        "report2dekrow7417",
        "test7417worked2",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked2)) == 6
    if text_length < 0 or token_count < 0:
        return False, token_count, "token-count"
    if empty_tokens > 0:
        return False, empty_tokens, "empty-token"
    if not offsets_valid:
        return False, token_count, "offset-contract"
    if not word_policy_named:
        return False, token_count, "undefined-word"
    assert token_count <= text_length
    return True, token_count, "bounded-tokens"

content.code:
# Evidence: bookbot-token-offsets-1uxksx
# Competency: bookbot-token-offsets.
# Case 4325: an offline public-library reading report.
# Operating constraint: test empty, malformed, Unicode, tied, large, changed-policy, and rejected cases before accepting output.
# Changed case: install the built wheel in a fresh environment and rerun success, invalid-argument, and decode-failure cases.
def evidence_bookbot_token_offsets_1uxksx(text_length: int = 42, token_count: int = 7, empty_tokens: int = 0, offsets_valid: bool = True, word_policy_named: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1uxksx = "1uxksx-xskxu1-4325"
    assert evidence_variant_1uxksx
    evidence_axes_1uxksx = (
        "corpus1uxksx",
        "policyxskxu1",
        "revision4325",
        "command1uxksx4325",
        "reportxskxu14325",
        "test43251uxksx",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1uxksx)) == 6
    if text_length < 0 or token_count < 0:
        return False, token_count, "token-count"
    if empty_tokens > 0:
        return False, empty_tokens, "empty-token"
    if not offsets_valid:
        return False, token_count, "offset-contract"
    if not word_policy_named:
        return False, token_count, "undefined-word"
    assert token_count <= text_length
    return True, token_count, "bounded-tokens"

content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Return original start and end offsets with normalized token keys so count evidence can be inspected in source.

At Bookbot and Python Defend the Token Offsets model · Theory Token Offsets, trace this concrete input and predict admission, decoding, normalization, tokenization, counting, ranking, formatting, stdout, stderr, status, test, package, failure, and recovery evidence before reviewing it: A theory Bookbot team handles case 8281 during an assistive-technology terminal review. Build deterministic Python evidence to tokenize under a named word policy with nonempty tokens and valid source offsets; preserve original text and offsets beside every normalized comparison key and report exclusion; then replace an ASCII word with a canonically equivalent combining sequence and compare the declared counts. Browser code uses pure bounded functions and original fixed fixtures only. Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Return original start and end offsets with normalized token keys so count evidence can be inspected in source.

For Bookbot and Python Defend the Token Offsets model · Theory Token Offsets, challenge the documented misconception with changed-file, changed-encoding, Unicode, token, ranking, stream, CLI, test, package, security, accessibility, failure, recovery, and controlled native-transfer evidence: A normalized token value is enough to locate its occurrence.

checks.failureMessage: Add the exact evidence marker # Evidence: bookbot-token-offsets-1uxksx.

checks.pattern: # Evidence: bookbot-token-offsets-1uxksx(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?def\s+evidence_bookbot_token_offsets_1uxksx\s*\((?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?\)\s*(?:->[^:]+)?\s*:(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?text_length < 0)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?empty_tokens > 0)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?not offsets_valid)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?not word_policy_named)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?assert)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return)(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return
